title: Browser Isolation Usage
app: Menlo Security syslog, `Splunk_TA_zscaler` (RBI), Island Enterprise Browser syslog, Broadcom Symantec WSS Add-on (Splunkbase 3856), Forcepoint ONE syslog
spl:
index=zt sourcetype="rbi:session" earliest=-30d
| eval isolated=if(match(lower(session_type),"(?i)isolated|rbi"),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(isolated) as isolated_sessions, count as total_sessions
| eval isolation_rate=round(100*isolated_sessions/total_sessions,1)
description: Measures adoption of remote browser isolation (RBI) sessions vs direct access — for licensing and risky-site coverage.
value: Security teams track browser isolation usage patterns — why sessions are isolated, how many users are protected, and what data transfer attempts occur within isolated sessions — to measure the effectiveness of clientless threat prevention and identify data exfiltration attempts.
implementation: Map vendor-specific session types. Alert when isolation_rate drops vs baseline for high-risk categories.
visualization: Line chart (isolation rate), Bar chart (sessions by app), Single value (% isolated).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Browser isolation platform logs. Zscaler Cloud Browser Isolation (CBI): `sourcetype=zscaler:zia:isolation`. Cloudflare Browser Isolation: embedded in `sourcetype=cloudflare:logpush:http_requests` with isolation-specific fields. Menlo Security: `sourcetype=menlo:web`. Key fields: `user`, `url`, `domain`, `isolation_reason`, `isolation_profile`, `action` (isolated/allowed/blocked), `data_transfer` (upload/download/clipboard).
• Browser Isolation renders web content in a remote cloud container, sending only safe pixels to the user's browser. It protects against zero-day browser exploits, drive-by downloads, and phishing credential theft.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify isolation events:
```spl
index=zscaler sourcetype="zscaler:zia:isolation" earliest=-7d
| stats count by isolation_reason
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Isolation usage overview:
```spl
index=zscaler sourcetype="zscaler:zia:isolation" earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(user) as users dc(domain) as domains by isolation_reason, isolation_profile
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL: Shows why sessions are being isolated (risk-based, URL category, policy, user request). If most isolations are 'risk-based' (unknown or suspicious sites), the system is working as designed. If 'policy' isolations are very high, the policy may be too broad. 'User request' shows adoption of user-initiated isolation.

Data transfer control:
```spl
index=zscaler sourcetype="zscaler:zia:isolation" earliest=-7d
| where data_transfer IN ("upload", "download", "clipboard", "print")
| stats count by user, data_transfer, domain
| sort - count
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Visit a site in the isolation category. Verify the isolation event.
(b) Attempt a file download inside isolation. Verify the data_transfer event.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Isolation sessions trend. Row 2 — Isolation by reason. Row 3 — Data transfer attempts.
Alert: When data transfer (upload) from isolation exceeds threshold.

Runbook:
1. **High upload attempts** — Users trying to extract data from isolated sessions. Review DLP policy.
2. **Isolation volume spike** — New URL category added to isolation profile. Expected.
3. **Low adoption** — Users may not understand isolation. Send awareness communication.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No isolation events** — CBI may not be licensed or enabled. Check Zscaler Admin.
• **`isolation_reason` empty** — Event format may differ by CBI version. Check field extraction.
grandmaExplanation: Instead of blocking risky websites outright, we open them in a safe sandbox in the cloud and send only a picture to the user's screen. We track how often this happens and watch for anyone trying to copy sensitive information out of these sandboxed sessions.
